summary: The Kleaning Kompany provides B2B commercial cleaning on recurring janitorial contracts — offices, medical facilities, commercial kitchens, HOAs, schools, retail centers, and specialty floor care — in South Florida and select multi-state markets. Every engagement is custom-scoped after a free site inspection, then billed on a daily, weekly, or monthly cadence. It serves facilities directors, property managers, and business owners who select and oversee vendors for their commercial spaces.

campaigns.name: Proposal-to-Contract Conversion
campaigns.objective: Turn delivered custom proposals into signed contracts by neutralizing the trust-and-reliability objection and removing friction from signing.
campaigns.trigger: Site inspection completed and custom proposal delivered; proposal not yet signed.
campaigns.segment: Prospects with a delivered proposal awaiting signature; branch by service type from the contact-form dropdown (office, medical, kitchen, HOA, school, retail, specialty floor care).
campaigns.rationale: Closing inspected prospects is the brand's stated #1 goal and its single biggest revenue leak — the deal lives or dies on this send, and trust is the dominant blocker that this email is built to dissolve.

emails.purpose: Deliver the custom proposal, directly answer the trust objection, and make signing the path of least resistance.
emails.subject: Your custom cleaning proposal is ready — here's how we earn your trust
emails.preview: Walk through the scope line by line, see why 500+ South Florida facilities switched, and sign only when you're ready.
keyPoints: Deliver the proposal scoped to exactly what was seen during the site walkthrough — frequency, square footage, and the specific service type they requested
Meet the trust objection head-on: vetted, insured, background-checked crews; supervised work; secure key handling; and a 98% client retention rate
Anchor credibility with 500+ satisfied clients and 10+ years serving commercial facilities, leaning on the 'I wish I'd switched sooner' switching narrative
Lower the stakes — no long lock-in surprises, custom terms, and a real person who replies within ~2 hours to answer any line item before signing

campaigns.name: New-Client Welcome & First-Clean Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Set expectations for a freshly signed contract and prime the client to notice and celebrate the first immaculate morning — protecting retention from day one.
campaigns.segment: Newly contracted clients; branch by facility type to tailor what to expect (medical, kitchen, office, HOA, school, retail).
campaigns.rationale: The 98% retention rate is won or lost on the first impression, and there is no onboarding today; this email confirms cadence and contact so a new client feels confident before the crew ever shows up.

emails.purpose: Warmly welcome the new client, confirm the service schedule and point of contact, and set up the first-clean 'aha' moment.
emails.subject: Welcome to The Kleaning Kompany — what to expect before your first clean
emails.preview: Your schedule, your dedicated contact, and the morning you'll walk in to a spotless facility.
keyPoints: Confirm the agreed cleaning cadence (daily / weekly / monthly) and the date of the first scheduled service so there are zero surprises
Introduce a named account manager and the ~2-hour response promise, so the client knows exactly who to reach and how fast
Prime the 'aha' — tell them to notice that first morning walking in to an immaculate facility, the moment 500+ clients say confirmed their switch
Reassure on security and supervision: vetted, insured crews, careful key/alarm handling, and (for medical/kitchen) adherence to OSHA/CDC sanitation standards

campaigns.name: Free Site Inspection Confirmation & Reminders
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended site inspections and cut no-shows so the proposal-to-contract engine stays fed.
campaigns.trigger: Free site inspection booked via form or phone.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled site walkthrough; branch by service type to tailor what the inspector will assess.
campaigns.rationale: Every contract starts with an attended inspection, so a missed walkthrough is lost pipeline; confirmation-plus-reminders recover roughly a third of would-be no-shows at almost no cost.

emails.purpose: Instantly confirm the booked walkthrough, reduce appointment anxiety, and set expectations so the prospect shows up prepared (and reassured).
emails.subject: You're confirmed — your free site walkthrough is set
emails.preview: Here's who's stopping by, what we'll look at, and how to prep (you really don't need to).
keyPoints: Confirm the date, time, and that the walkthrough is genuinely free with no obligation to sign
Name the inspector who's coming and frame the visit as quick and low-effort — they just walk us through the space
Set the payoff: they'll receive a custom, line-by-line proposal scoped to their facility, not a generic quote
Include an easy reschedule link and a contact for questions, with the ~2-hour response promise to build trust before the first meeting

campaigns.name: Unconverted Quote-Inquiry Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move quote inquiries that didn't book or sign immediately back toward a free site inspection by dissolving the switching-vendor fear with proof.
campaigns.trigger: Contact-form inquiry with no inspection booked or proposal signed after first touch.
campaigns.segment: Cold/stalled inquiries below sales-ready threshold; branch by the service-type dropdown they selected.
campaigns.rationale: Inbound inquiries that go cold are a recoverable, no-extra-acquisition-cost source of contracts, and the only thing usually standing between them and a booking is trust — exactly what social proof fixes.

emails.purpose: Name the switching-vendor anxiety, prove reliability with social proof, and re-invite to the zero-risk free inspection.
emails.subject: Switching cleaning vendors feels risky — here's how 500+ facilities did it without a hiccup
emails.preview: Vetted, insured crews, a 98% retention rate, and a free walkthrough before you commit to anything.
keyPoints: Lead with the real hesitation: the fear of repeating a bad experience with an unreliable prior cleaning company
Counter it with hard proof — 500+ satisfied South Florida clients, 98% retention, and 10+ years across offices, medical, kitchens, and HOAs
Reframe the inspection as completely risk-free: see the proposal first, commit only if it's right for the facility
Reassure on the unsupervised-access worry with vetted, insured, background-checked, and supervised crews

campaigns.name: Anniversary Recap & Service Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect recurring contract revenue and grow accounts by reaffirming the value delivered and introducing specialty add-on services.
campaigns.trigger: Contract anniversary, or a usage/footprint milestone signaling expansion readiness.
campaigns.segment: Active contracted clients approaching their anniversary; branch by health — high-satisfaction accounts get the expansion offer, at-risk accounts get a service review.
campaigns.rationale: With 98% retention already strong, the bigger lever is account growth — moving base janitorial clients into specialty floor care, kitchen cleaning, or added days — and the anniversary is the natural, non-salesy moment to ask.

emails.purpose: Recap a year of reliable service, thank the client, and surface the highest-fit expansion services plus a referral ask.
emails.subject: One year of spotless mornings — here's what we can take off your plate next
emails.preview: A quick recap of your year with us, plus three ways to do even more for your facility.
keyPoints: Quantify the year — number of completed cleans, consistency maintained, and special requests handled — to make the value tangible
Thank them for trusting us with their facility and reaffirm the reliability that earned the contract
Introduce best-fit expansion services: specialty floor stripping/waxing, carpet extraction, commercial kitchen cleaning, or additional service days
Embed a warm referral ask — if their mornings have been spotless, invite them to introduce a fellow facilities manager
